title: T with stroke
summary: Ŧ (lowercase: ŧ, Latin alphabet), known as T with stroke or T with bar, is the 25th letter in the Northern Sámi alphabet, where it represents the voiceless dental fricative [θ]. In the SENĆOŦEN alphabet, it represents [s̪]. It is also used in the Hualapai alphabet, as well as in several orthographies for African languages, e.g., for Hassaniya Arabic in Senegal.  The Unicode codepoints for this letter are U+0166 Ŧ LATIN CAPITAL LETTER T WITH STROKE and U+0167 ŧ LATIN SMALL LETTER T WITH STROKE. Other letters with a stroke include ǥ, ħ, đ, ł, and ø.
text:
Ŧ (lowercase: ŧ, Latin alphabet), known as T with stroke or T with bar, is the 25th letter in the Northern Sámi alphabet, where it represents the voiceless dental fricative [θ]. In the SENĆOŦEN alphabet, it represents [s̪]. It is also used in the Hualapai alphabet, as well as in several orthographies for African languages, e.g., for Hassaniya Arabic in Senegal.  The Unicode codepoints for this letter are U+0166 Ŧ LATIN CAPITAL LETTER T WITH STROKE and U+0167 ŧ LATIN SMALL LETTER T WITH STROKE. Other letters with a stroke include ǥ, ħ, đ, ł, and ø.
